title: Battle of Tatooine (Galactic Civil War)
content:

Assault Corvette/Legends|''Marauder''-class corvettes]] *[[EF76 Nebulon-B escort frigate/Legends|Nebulon-B escort frigates]] *[[Assault Frigate Mark II/Legends|Assault Frigate Mark IIs]] *[[MC80 Liberty Type Heavy Star Cruiser/Legends|MC80 ''Liberty'' type Star Cruiser]] |forces3=*[[Rihkxyrk assault fighter]]s *[[Interceptor-class frigate/Legends|''Interceptor''-class frigates]] *1 [[asteroid base]] |forces4= |casual1=*Many troopers **Mod Terrik *Many AT-STs *1 Immobilizer 418 cruiser *1 ''Imperial''-class Star Destroyer heavily damaged *Minor damages to the fleet |casual2=*''Tantive IV'' and its crew, including Captain [[Raymus Antilles/Legends|Raymus Antilles]] *Most Rebel infantry *Many starships damaged or destroyed *[[Anchorhead Base]] |casual3=*All Black Sun forces *Many Tuskens |casual4= |civilian=*[[Owen Lars/Legends|Owen]] and [[Beru Whitesun Lars/Legends|Beru Lars]] *1 [[Jawa camp]] *[[Nebit's tribe/Legends|Nebit's clan]] }} {{Quote|That's a lot of firepower for a planet like Tatooine.|[[Karoly D'ulin]]|{{StoryCite|story=Hammertong: The Tale of the "Tonnika Sisters"|book=Tales from the Mos Eisley Cantina}}}} The '''Battle of Tatooine''' in the [[Galactic Civil War/Legends|Galactic Civil War]] was a series of battles and skirmishes in the [[Tatoo system/Legends|Tatoo system]], primarily on the planet [[Tatooine/Legends|Tatooine]], within a few days before the climactic [[Battle of Yavin/Legends|Battle of Yavin]]. It resulted in the capture of [[Princess/Legends|Princess]] [[Leia Organa Solo/Legends|Leia Organa]] by [[Anakin Skywalker/Legends|Darth Vader]]. Many of the local [[Alliance to Restore the Republic/Legends|Rebel]] enemies of the [[Galactic Empire/Legends|Galactic Empire]] were captured or killed, with most operations being carried out in parts by the [[Desert Sands]] and the renowned [[501st Legion/Legends|501st Legion]] of [[Stormtrooper/Legends|stormtroopers]]. ==Prelude== {{Quote|With the information gathered on Polis Massa, Vader concluded that the stolen plans had been given to Princess Leia Organa of Alderaan. We weren't surprised. For all their talk of being a peaceful planet, Alderaan had been thumbing its nose at the Empire for years.|[[Retired clone trooper|Unidentified 501st stormtrooper]]|}} The battle was part of the [[Galactic Civil War/Legends|Galactic Civil War]], a major conflict that opposed the [[Galactic Empire/Legends|Galactic Empire]] and the [[Alliance to Restore the Republic/Legends|Alliance to Restore the Republic]]. An unwitting instigator to the battle was the disguised [[Alderaan/Legends|Alderaanian]] diplomatic [[Corvette/Legends|corvette]] [[Tantive IV/Legends|''Tantive IV'']], being used as an important vessel in the Rebel Alliance fleet, most notably in the operation to steal the [[Death Star plans/Legends|plans]] of the [[DS-1 Orbital Battle Station/Legends|Death Star]]: the new Imperial [[Superweapon/Legends|superweapon]]. The theft of these plans was the aim of the wider [[Operation Skyhook]]. It successfully occurred during a distracting [[Death Star Uprising|Rebel prisoner revolt]] on the Death Star, being supplemented by partial documents stolen during the [[Mission to Danuta|Battle of Danuta]] and the [[Battle of Toprawa]]. Immediately, an [[Raid on Polis Massa|Imperial assault]] on a makeshift Rebel base on [[Polis Massa/Legends|Polis Massa]] was initiated, being tasked with retaking the stolen plans. This, however, failed in recovering the lost schematics. Nonetheless, the information they did receive on Polis Massa caused them to conclude that the [[Alderaanian/Legends|Alderaanian]] [[Senator/Legends|Senator]] and [[Princess/Legends|Princess]] [[Leia Organa Solo/Legends|Leia Organa]] was involved in the theft. As Alderaan had been a nuisance to the Empire for [[Standard year/Legends|years]], it came as little shock to the 501st legion. The next attempt in the recovery effort involved the [[Galactic Emperor/Legends|Emperor]] [[Palpatine/Legends|Palpatine's]] feared enforcer, [[Sith Lord/Legends|Sith Lord]] [[Anakin Skywalker/Legends|Darth Vader]], taking his legion of Imperial stormtroopers, the [[501st Legion/Legends|501st Legion]], to where the ''Tantive IV'' was harbored—within the Tatoo system. They managed to track the ''Tantive IV'' with the unknowing aid of the protocol droid [[U-3PO/Legends|U-3PO]], who had earlier been programmed to act as a [[Sleeper agent/Legends|sleeper agent]] and covertly placed on the ship's droid pool on Ralltiir.{{SWArchive|url=episode-iv/explore/visualguides/chp03.html|text=Episode IV Visual Guide — Chapter 3: Imperial Boarding Party}} The information that the ''Tantive IV'' held the plans had been obtained by captured Rebels, as well as a [[Bothan/Legends|Bothan]] who revealed it to the Emperor on [[Bothawui/Legends|Bothawui]]. ==The battle== ===Recovering the plans=== {{Quote|Where are those transmissions you intercepted? What have you done with those plans?!''"
"''We intercepted no transmissions…! This is a consular ship…! We're on a…diplomatic mission…|[[Anakin Skywalker/Legends|Darth Vader]] and [[Raymus Antilles/Legends|Captain Antilles]]|}} [[File:Devastatortantiveiv.png|thumb|left|180px|The Star Destroyer ''Devastator'' chases down the ''Tantive IV'' over Tatooine.]] Upon reaching the Tatoo system, the Imperial fleet was ambushed by a [[Pirate/Legends|pirate]] fleet near the planet Tatooine. Upon taking out most of the pirate attackers, the Navy then hid in the surrounding nebulas, avoiding detection by Rebel Alliance [[Blue Squadron (Rebel Alliance)/Legends|Blue Squadron]], led by [[Raymus Antilles/Legends|Raymus Antilles]], who were scouting the area. When the ''Tantive IV'' moved toward the uplink station, [[Immobilizer 418 Interdictor-class Heavy Cruiser|''Interdictor''-class cruisers]] were activated, preventing it from escaping. A clash between the Imperial fleet and the Alliance fleet broke out, with both sides taking several casualties in the crossfire. The [[Star Destroyer/Legends|Star Destroyer]] [[Devastator/Legends|''Devastator'']] pursued the ''Tantive IV'' out of the battlefield and chased it around the planet Tatooine, Vader's homeworld.{{Film|I}} {{Noncanonstart|legends=1}} The ''Devastator'' captured what they thought was the ''Tantive IV'' in a [[Tractor beam/Legends|tractor beam]], but it turned out to be just a decoy called the [[Tantive V (CR90 corvette)|''Tantive V'']]. After searching the ship and finding no one except Gungans, the ''Tantive V'' was set adrift into space and the ''Devastator'' continued to pursue the ''Tantive IV''.{{StoryCite|story=Tantive IV (comic story)|book=Star Wars Manga: Black|stext=''Tantive IV''}} {{Noncanonend}} A laser bolt hit near the engine section of the ''Tantive IV'', and the ship was captured by the ''Devastator''{{'s}} [[Tractor beam/Legends|tractor beams]]. In an effort to avoid destruction, the corvette signalled its surrender and shut down the main [[Reactor/Legends|reactor]].''[[The Essential Guide to Weapons and Technology]]'' Vader then dispatched a [[TIE/br boarding shuttle|TIE boarding craft]] filled up with troops, to board and secure the ship, however, the crew of the destroyer attached a portable accessway to the corvette instead. ===Inside the Tantive IV=== {{Quote|Retrieve the plans and bring the ambassador to Lord Vader—alive.|Instructions given to all 501st stormtroopers, prior to the battle on the ''Tantive IV''|}} [[File:Tantive_IV_Battlefront_II_promo.jpg|thumb|right|220px|Rebel troopers try to repel the Stormtrooper assault aboard ''Tantive IV''.]] As troopers of the [[501st Legion/Legends|501st Legion]] stormed the hallways of the ''Tantive IV'', a skirmish erupted between them and [[Rebel trooper/Legends|Rebel troopers]] defending the ship, with [[Unidentified 501st stormtrooper (first casualty)|one of the stormtroopers]] being killed just as he boarded the corvette. Due to the use of [[Grenade/Legends|grenades]], both sides incurred heavy casualties, but the 501st broke through, capturing the main Rebel barracks and clearing the way for Vader's arrival. Vader himself was personally displeased with Tatooine being the location of their capture of the ''Tantive IV'', due to it being his hated homeworld and it bringing back bad memories of the [[Mission to rescue Shmi Skywalker Lars/Legends|last time]] he saw his [[Shmi Skywalker Lars/Legends|mother]].''[[Star Wars: The Complete Visual Dictionary]]'' He personally destroyed all security terminals on the bridge, in order to send the defenses into disarray, and disabled a turbine console to steal a set of access codes, in an attempt to gain entry to the ship's escape pod bays, before he and a set of Imperial bodyguards ventured out through the corridors, killing some Rebel troopers and capturing many more. The team then stormed the cockpit of the corvette. During the initial [[lightfight]], the [[Astromech droid/Legends|astromech droid]] R2-D2 and protocol droid C-3PO slipped through the confusion, but eventually ended up separated. R2-D2 then met with the ship's ambassador, Alderaanian Senator and Princess [[Leia Organa Solo/Legends|Leia Organa]]. Anticipating that she was cornered with no option of escape, Organa ordered the astromech to use the escape pods to ensure the plans weren't recovered by the Imperials, and managed to record a SOS transmission intended for Kenobi, requesting for his assistance in delivering the stolen plans to her father, [[Bail Prestor Organa/Legends|Bail Organa]], on Alderaan. Sensing someone arriving, she then hastily placed the datadisk containing all the pieces of the Death Star blueprints, and left just as C-3PO arrived looking for the R2 unit. R2 then suggested to C-3PO that he follow him to the escape pod section. Most Rebel soldiers were captured, including the ship's captain, [[Raymus Antilles/Legends|Raymus Antilles]], whom Vader dealt with personally. While escorting said soldiers as well as searching the vessel, the Imperials had their droids [[R2-Q2/Legends|R2-Q2]] and [[R4-M9/Legends|R4-M9]] scour the ship's databanks for the stolen Death Star plans. However, the 501st [[Unidentified stormtrooper commander (Tantive IV)|commander]] reported to Vader that the stolen plans were no longer on the ship's main computer. This resulted in Vader interrogating Antilles by strangling him, asking about their transmission interceptions relating to the plans, and what they'd done with them. Antilles attempted to deny any interceptions and that the ''Tantive IV'' was on a diplomatic mission due to being a consular ship. Vader then questioned where the ambassador was, but Antilles died before he could answer, his neck having been crushed. The Sith Lord then threw the captain's body against a wall in anger. [[File:Leia_Organa_by_Anthony_Foti.jpg|thumb|left|Princess Leia Organa escapes Imperial forces.]] Vader then proceeded to order the 501st Legion to search for the missing plans along with locating the ship's passengers—including the ambassador—and bring them to him alive. After giving this command, Vader utilized an IT-O interrogator droid on several of the captured soldiers to extract any information they had of the Death Star plans. As the 501st Legion searched the ship, they managed to find the ambassador, Organa, who shot [[Unidentified 501st stormtrooper|one of the oncoming stormtroopers]] in the chest, killing him. She was stunned immediately after by [[DV-692]]. Vader personally confronted her, and ordered for her arrest under the charge of [[Treason/Legends|treason]], unaware at the time that she was his own daughter. Shortly afterward, Commander [[Daine Jir/Legends|Daine Jir]] protested Vader's decision, citing that it carried too much risk of generating sympathy among the Imperial Senate towards her plight and thus worsen the situation regarding their fight against the Rebels, although Vader reminded him that she was their only link to the [[Great Temple/Legends|secret Rebel Base]]. When Jir pointed out she's more likely to die than tell him anything, Vader assured him to leave it to him to get it out of her, before promptly ordering for a distress signal to be sent to [[Wide-Eyed|nearby vessels]], and then report that the ''Tantive IV'' hit a meteor storm and all onboard had been killed due to the ship's destruction. In addition, the ''Devastator'' also destroyed any escape pods that were utilized by any escaping Rebel personnel. The Rebel protocol droid [[C-3PO/Legends|C-3PO]], along with [[R2-D2/Legends|R2-D2]], an astromech droid vital to the Rebellion, subsequently escaped the ship: R2-D2 having secretly obtained the Death Star schematics Vader had been so eager to recover. R2-D2 chose to escape via an escape pod despite droids being forbidden, both due to Leia's orders, and due to prior experience in the [[Karthakk system|Maramere system]].{{SW|url=news/the-droids-re-animated-part-1|series=The Droids Re-Animated|text=Part 1|nolive=1}} The ''Devastator'' also prepared to destroy the escape pod C-3PO and R2-D2 were on, but stopped when they realized that it contained no life signs, and subsequently dismissed it as a malfunction. Nonetheless, they reported the incident to Vader via Commander [[Nahdonnis Praji]] shortly after ordering Jir to send the fake distress signal, who deduced that the escape pod must have been where Leia stashed the Death Star plans, and ordered them to search Tatooine for the plans, and for Praji specifically to personally see to it as well. The planet Tatooine had already been [[Scout/Legends|scouted]] some time ago by Imperial operatives who had not reported any Rebel presence on the world.[[Death Star (novel)|''Death Star'']] Around that time, the smuggler Han Solo arrived at Tatooine in his starship the [[Millennium Falcon/Legends|''Millennium Falcon'']]. On his way to landing he destroyed multiple TIE fighters. [[File:StarDestroyerAttack-RebelAssault.png|thumb|right|200px|Rookie One takes on a Star Destroyer.]] Meanwhile, a distress signal was sent to a [[Kolaador Base|Rebel base]] on [[Kolaador]]. Soon after the capture of the ''Tantive IV'', a small [[Squadron/Legends|squadron]] of [[T-65 X-wing starfighter/Legends|X-wings]] arrived from Kolaador. Commander [[Ru Murleen]], [[Rookie One]] and [[Thurlow Harris]], flew their fighters in space above Tatooine and turned their weapons towards an ''Imperial''-class Star Destroyer. Using their [[Proton torpedo/Legends|proton torpedoes]], they managed to strike a critical blow and headed to the surface. However they were not in time to prevent three [[TIE/ln space superiority starfighter/Legends|TIE/LN starfighters]] from attacking and destroying their [[Anchorhead Base|base]], located near [[Anchorhead/Legends|Anchorhead]]. Acting quickly to prevent the TIEs reporting their presence back to their commanders, Rookie One gave chase through [[Beggar's Canyon/Legends|Beggar's Canyon]] and destroyed all three Imperial fighters. Captain [[Merrick Simms]] survived the destruction of the Anchorhead base flying in his X-wing and then joined the three other Rebels. ===Desert Extermination=== {{Quote|She must have hidden the plans in the escape pod. Send a detachment down to retrieve them… See to it personally Commander.|Darth Vader, to Commander Praji|}} [[File:Stormtrooper_Search.png|thumb|left|220px|Desert Sands searches for the Death Star plans.]] Darth Vader ordered [[Commander/Legends|Commander]] [[Nahdonnis Praji|Praji]] to lead personally the search the Death Star plans on Tatooine's surface. He assigned Captain [[Kosh/Legends|Kosh's]] local [[Garrison/Legends|garrison]] to the task, while Commander [[Daine Jir/Legends|Jir's]] 501st was readied. These events coincided with the arrival of the Star Destroyer [[Inquisitor (Star Destroyer)|''Inquisitor'']] in the system.{{Hyperspace|url=hyperspace/source/praji/20090205.html|text=Tinker, Tailor, Soldier, Praji|int=Tinker, Tailor, Soldier, Praji}} Under command of [[General/Legends|General]] [[Malcor Brashin]], the ''Inquisitor'' was carrying Captain [[Mod Terrik|Terrik]] and his [[Desert Sands]] for training exercises and eventually to relieve the [[37th Detachment]] stationed on the planet. Primarily, the Star Destroyer accompanied the Desert Sands to test the experimental [[Battlefield Holographic Control Interface]] during the exercises of Captain Terrik's troopers. Alerted to the situation, the Desert Sands quickly landed on the planet to undertake the search for the Rebel corvette's [[Escape pod/Legends|escape pod]]. Split into [[Alvien Squadron|Alvien]], [[Drax Squadron|Drax]] and [[Zeta Squadron (Desert Sands)|Zeta Squadrons]], the [[Desert stormtrooper/Legends|sandtroopers]] explored the deserts of Tatooine for hours. Terrik personally commanded Zeta Squadron which was assisted by [[Dewback/Legends|dewbacks]], one [[HAVr A9 Floating Fortress/Legends|HAVr A9 floating fortress]] and two [[Arunskin 32 cargo skiff]]s from the local garrison. [[File:Tatooinebattle.jpg|thumb|right|220px|A Rebel trooper attacks an Imperial AT-ST.]] As Imperial sandtroopers attempted to construct command stations in the [[Dune Sea/Legends|Dune Sea]], they were assaulted by the fierce, nomadic [[Tusken Raider/Legends|Tusken Raiders]]. Soon after, Rebel soldiers appeared and a three-way battle broke out near the Tusken camps stationed around a fierce [[Sarlacc/Legends|Sarlacc]]. The Rebels were able to deploy some [[Rebel combat speeder]]s and X-wings to support their troops but the small army was completely eradicated by Imperial forces. The crashed escape pod was eventually found in the Dune Sea by [[Davin Felth/Legends|Davin Felth]] of Zeta Squadron. The sandtroopers discovered evidence that the pod's occupants were [[Droid/Legends|droids]], in the form of a piece of metal that fell off from [[R2-D2/Legends|R2-D2]].{{StoryCite|story=When the Desert Wind Turns: The Stormtrooper's Tale|book=Tales from the Mos Eisley Cantina}} They followed a trail leading away from the escape pod and discovered that the droids had been intercepted by a [[Jawa/Legends|Jawa]] [[Sandcrawler/Legends|sandcrawler]]. They eventually intercepted the [[digger crawler]], where they learned from the [[Nebit's tribe/Legends|Jawas]] that the droids had been already sold to [[Moisture farmer/Legends|moisture farmers]]. Captain Terrik then ordered his men to attack the sandcrawler, leaving no survivors among the Jawas. To cover up the Imperial attack on the Jawas, they left gaffi sticks nearby in order to implicate a [[Tusken Raider/Legends|Tusken Raider]] raid as being responsible for the attack. The Imperials then largely destroyed the Sandcrawler with a [[Reconnaissance Troop Transporter]].[[Imperial troop transporter (story booklet)|Imperial troop transporter story booklet]] [[File:PlunkettC4print.jpg|thumb|left|220px|Sandtroopers attack the Jawa sandcrawler.]] The Imperials then proceeded to pay a visit to the [[Lars homestead/Legends|moisture farm]] that had purchased the droids. Its inhabitants, [[Owen Lars/Legends|Owen]] and [[Beru Whitesun Lars/Legends|Beru Lars]], not only refused to hand the droids over, but their patriarch, Owen Lars, also spat at Terrik. The soldiers, under Vader's personal orders via [[Hologram/Legends|hologram]], noticed that a landspeeder was missing from their garage. The Sith Lord ordered the establishing of checkpoints to detain any droids entering [[Mos Espa/Legends|Mos Espa]] or [[Mos Eisley/Legends|Mos Eisley]] spaceports. Vader eventually watched the troopers carry out his orders on their helpless victims.''[[The Rise and Fall of Darth Vader]]'' They executed Lars and Beru and cremated their remains. The droids were in fact in possession of their nephew: [[Luke Skywalker/Legends|Luke Skywalker]]. After this new failure, Captain Terrik ordered the troopers to regroup in the city of Mos Eisley, the only way to leave the planet for any fugitive. ===Siege of Mos Eisley=== {{Quote|Local smugglers from Mos Eisley have escaped with the Rebel droids. It is time to bring Imperial order to Tatooine. Seize control of Mos Eisley and terminate any who resist.|Emperor Palpatine's briefing before the Mos Eisley mission|}} [[File:Tatbattle2.jpg|thumb|right|200px|Sandtroopers plow through Mos Eisley.]] Luke Skywalker and the droids eventually arrived in Mos Eisley, a [[Spaceport/Legends|spaceport]] renowned as a magnet for outcasts, crooks, and intergalactic escapees. With their new unexpected ally, [[Jedi Master/Legends|Jedi Master]] [[Obi-Wan Kenobi/Legends|Obi-Wan Kenobi]], they wished to find a [[Transport/Legends|transport]] in order to leave the planet and bring the Death Star plans to [[Alderaan/Legends|Alderaan]]. But Mos Eisley was believed to be the refuge of the fugitive droids dispatched by the Rebellion. With the stolen schematics in its databanks, R2-D2 had become a high-priority Imperial target and Imperial sandtroopers scattered throughout the city, to retrieve information about the stolen plans, with [[Alvien Squadron]] in particular manning various checkpoints. Ironically, the sandtroopers of Alvien Squadron ended up unwittingly allowing the wanted droids to pass by, thanks to a [[Mind trick/Legends|mind trick]] placed by Obi-Wan Kenobi. [[File:Sandtroopers2-hd.png|thumb|left|180px|Zeta Squadron marches toward Docking Bay 94.]] Luke Skywalker, Obi-Wan Kenobi, and the droids made their way to the [[Chalmun's Spaceport Cantina/Legends|Mos Eisley cantina]] in order to look for a [[Pilot/Legends|pilot]] to the [[Alderaan system/Legends|Alderaan system]]. There, they finally met with the pilot who would soon fly them: [[Han Solo/Legends|Han Solo]], and his copilot [[Chewbacca/Legends|Chewbacca]]. In the cantina, Skywalker encountered the [[Aqualish/Legends|Aqualish]] scoundrel [[Ponda Baba/Legends|Ponda Baba]], who threatened to kill him. In defense of the young man, Kenobi was forced to use his [[Lightsaber/Legends|lightsaber]], [[Cho mai/Legends|removing]] Baba's arm. A patron, [[Garouf Lafoe/Legends|Garouf Lafoe]], rushed to notify the Imperial troops upon witnessing a scuffle including a man wielding a lightsaber. Upon the troops' arrival, Luke and Obi-Wan had already vacated the premises. Meanwhile, the [[Spy/Legends|spy]] [[Garindan/Legends|Garindan]] informed the stormtroopers of the droids' location, and that the smuggler Han Solo was helping the droids escape Tatooine. Captain Mod Terrik and Zeta Squadron moved into [[Docking Bay 94/Legends|Docking Bay 94]], where they engaged Solo in a firefight, only to have him escape. During the firefight, Terrik was gunned down by one of his men, Imperial traitor and Rebel sympathizer [[Davin Felth/Legends|Davin Felth]]. The ''Falcon'' then flew through the [[Dune Sea/Legends|Dune Sea]], destroying several [[Viper probe droid/Legends|probe droids]] in order to keep its position unknown from the orbital Star Destroyers. After the [[Millennium Falcon/Legends|''Millennium Falcon'']]{{'s}} escape, a "pacification" campaign was waged in the streets. Several Mos Eisley citizens, along with Rebels, managed to secure run-down apartment districts and a damaged [[Sail barge/Legends|sail barge]] among other locales. The Stormtroopers managed to squelch the resistance in short order. At some point, Merrick Simms and the pilots from Kolaador, proceeded to Mos Eisley where the Empire was deploying ground forces. While Murleen left to attempt to cut off the Imperial supply lines, Rookie One joined Simms and Harris in engaging [[All Terrain Scout Transport/Legends|All Terrain Scout Transports]] and Imperial [[Lambda-class T-4a shuttle/Legends|landing craft]]. After causing as much damage to the Imperial forces as they could, and with TIE fighters incoming, the group was forced to withdraw. Ultimately, the Rebels ended their hit-and-run attacks—most blasted dead before causing any real harm to Imperial soldiers—leaving the Empire victorious during this final operation. However, Vader never recovered the lost Death Star plans, as several Star Destroyers blockading the planet failed to capture ''Millennium Falcon'' as it fled to make contact with Bail Organa at Alderaan. Pursued by TIE fighters and interceptors, the pilots from Kolaador fled through the nearby [[Asteroid field/Legends|asteroid field]], losing their pursuers before rendezvousing with the fleet and proceeding to [[Gamma Base]] on the ice-world of [[Hoth/Legends|Hoth]]. ==Aftermath== {{Quote|Leave that to me. Send a distress signal. Then report that the ship was destroyed in an asteroid collision with all onboard killed.|[[Anakin Skywalker/Legends|Darth Vader]]|}} [[File:Falcon-tatooine.png|thumb|right|220px|The ''Millennium Falcon'' leaves Tatooine.]] Although the ''Millennium Falcon'' managed to elude their Imperial pursuers, they nonetheless failed in their objective of getting Kenobi to Alderaan, due to it being [[Destruction of Alderaan/Legends|blown away]] by the [[DS-1 Orbital Battle Station/Legends|Death Star's]] superlaser. The ''Falcon'' and its crew were later forced to dock in the gargantuan space station after mistaking it for a moon, and eventually commenced a [[Rescue of Princess Leia/Legends|rescue operation]] for Princess Leia Organa onboard the Death Star when planning a way to escape. The loss of the Death Star plans would ultimately result in the Rebel's amazing victory in the [[Battle of Yavin/Legends|Battle of Yavin]] not long after. Garindan later discovered in local records the name of a young man, [[Luke Skywalker/Legends|Luke Skywalker]], who sold his [[Landspeeder/Legends|landspeeder]] to leave the planet on the ''Millennium Falcon'', helping Vader to confirm the origins and identity of the Death Star's destroyer. Vader's decision to allow the Stormtroopers to kill the Lars family also resulted in their nephew Luke Skywalker joining the Rebel Alliance, an event that proved to turn the Galactic Civil War in the Rebels' favor, and ironically to the [[Battle of Endor/Legends|redemption]] of Vader four years afterward.{{Film|VI}} To hide their attack on ''Tantive IV'', Imperial officials declared that the pirate resistance over Tatooine had attacked the corvette, and a meteor shower had destroyed it. It was claimed the Imperial task force had simply gone to investigate. In addition, in order to ensure no one got suspicious, the Imperials, under Vader's command, also sent a fake distress signal from the ''Tantive IV'', and dispatched a survey ship, the ''[[Wide-Eyed]]'', to "confirm" the asteroid collision that doomed the ship and its passengers. It was only [[9 ABY/Legends|nine years later]] that the [[Bureau of Ships and Services/Legends|BoSS]] case on the ''Tantive IV's'' destruction was officially closed, and the Imperial report exposed as a complete fabrication.{{InsiderCite|90|Refresher Reading}} [[File:Exds.png|thumb|left|200px|The Death Star is destroyed.]] Likewise, a permanent [[Imperial garrison base]] was symbolically established in Mos Eisley as a result of the Battle of Yavin and the destruction of the Death Star."[[Tatooine Sojourn]]" The 501st detachment left [[Tatooine/Legends|Tatooine]] and returned to the Death Star where most died when the station was destroyed. Six months after the Battle of Yavin,''[[Star Wars: Rogue Squadron: The Official Nintendo Player's Guide]]'' the Empire decided to make a harsh maneuver — [[Viper probe droid/Legends|viper probe droids]] and [[TIE/sa tactical bomber/Legends|TIE Bombers]] were ordered to [[Ambush at Mos Eisley|harass]] homesteads and to execute a [[bombing run]] on Mos Eisley[[Star Wars: Rogue Squadron (video game)|''Star Wars: Rogue Squadron'']] to root out Luke Skywalker. Fortunately, Skywalker's newly-formed [[Rogue Squadron/Legends|Rogue Squadron]] happened to be training in the area of Beggar's Canyon at the time and were available for assistance. In the ensuing skirmish, Rogue Squadron was successful in intercepting and destroying the TIE Bombers before they could cause too much damage to the city. Despite this setback, the Empire would continue to maintain nominal control over Tatooine for the next few years. The citizens would finally be [[Rebellion on Tatooine|freed]] soon after the [[Battle of Endor/Legends|Battle of Endor]].{{Wizards|url=default.asp?x=starwars/article/sw20050512b|series=The Dark Forces Saga|text=Part 3: Pride of the Dark Side}} ==Behind the scenes== The space battle over Tatooine was created by [[George Lucas]] appeared first in the [[Star Wars: A New Hope (novelization)|novelization]] of [[Star Wars: Episode IV A New Hope|Episode IV ''A New Hope'']], ghostwritten by [[Alan Dean Foster]] and published in [[1976]]. The battle was later widely extended in the [[Star Wars Legends|Expanded Universe]], sometimes contradictorily, in the short story ''[[When the Desert Wind Turns: The Stormtrooper's Tale]]'' and in several [[Star Wars video games|video game]]s: ''[[Star Wars: Rebel Assault]]'', [[Star Wars: Force Commander|''Force Commander'']], [[Star Wars: Battlefront|''Battlefront'']] and [[Star Wars: Empire at War|''Empire at War'']]. The [[2009]] [[Hyperspace: The Official Star Wars Fan Club|Hyperspace]] article ''[[Tinker, Tailor, Soldier, Praji]]'' intended to reconcile those sources. The 2013 smartphone app ''[[Star Wars: Force Collection]]'' features the skirmish aboard the ''Tantive IV'' as its introduction, with Leia Organa asking the player, who is armed with a lightsaber, for
